description: Behavioral baseline + mechanistic localization across models (unquantized bf16, Modal A100): accuracy on the canonical-48 set (a+b*c=, a,b,c in 1..5, distinct), BODMAS-vs-LTR error split, engagement; logit-lens commit layer at =; per-layer MLP-vs-attention DLA; path-DLA (*->=) routing heads. Core methods in opprec/interp.py.
motivation: Establish the behavior exists, then localize where and which components resolve precedence, before attributing a circuit.
formal_results: Qwen2.5-Math-7B: 21/48 correct pre-FT, 48/48 post-FT (engagement 0.73 pre, 0.56 at b=5); Qwen-1.5B 13/48; DeepSeek-Math-7B 29/48; Llama-3.1-8B base 0/48 (cannot do the task). Precedence encoded in the = residual, late layers (commit ~L11-20). MLP 22-27 dominate DLA; path-DLA routing heads L22H13, L20H10, L27H11.
results_description: Math LMs resolve precedence at the = token in late layers via MLP 22-27 and a small set of routing heads; a non-math control (Llama) cannot do the task.
conclusions: The behavior exists and is localized to the = token in late layers; components are MLP 22-27 + routing heads - the substrate for the rule-vs-lookup question.

comments.provenance: Concrete numbers come from gitignored .modal_results/*.json (e.g. baseline_7B_pre.json); not re-derivable from git. Run on unquantized bf16 (the notebooks used 4-bit).
